description: Cosmic Daily Planner consolidated bibliography for runtime Oracle composition. Each entry has claim_tags (what the source supports) and voice_affinity (which voice should cite it). Used by /api/coordinate-drawer to compose voice-aware, citation-anchored Oracle prose per chip per coordinate. References verified via PubMed, peer-reviewed journals, and authoritative practitioner sources. Sceptical counterweights included intentionally so the bibliography reflects the empirical state, not only the favourable literature. | v1.4: expanded to full parity with the consolidated master; the runtime pool now mirrors the scholarly canon. Retrieval is by claim_to_entries index; the whole file is not injected per prompt. voice_chip_default_refs (default surfacing) is unchanged.

drayer-2002.title: Numerology: The Power in Numbers
drayer-2002.note: Practitioner depth on Pythagorean numerology. Symbolic framework, not empirical claim.

goodwin-1994.title: The Complete Numerology Guide
goodwin-1994.note: Reference text on Pythagorean numerology. Practitioner literature.

kahn-2001.title: Pythagoras and the Pythagoreans: A Brief History
kahn-2001.note: Authoritative scholarly history of Pythagorean philosophy. Provides historical-origin grounding distinct from the modern numerology interpretation.

riedweg-2005.title: Pythagoras: His Life, Teaching, and Influence
riedweg-2005.note: Peer-reviewed academic biography of Pythagoras. Distinguishes the historical figure from later esoteric interpretation.

burkert-1972.title: Lore and Science in Ancient Pythagoreanism
burkert-1972.note: The classical sceptical scholarship: distinguishes documented Pythagorean philosophy from later attributions. Useful when the Science voice acknowledges historical complexity.

bremer-2022.title: Default mode network, salience network, and the regulation of attention
bremer-2022.note: Review of network dynamics underlying shifts between reflective and executive cognitive states. Used to support claim that day-to-day signatures align with measurable cognitive bias, while flagging this as correlation not causation.

walker-2017.title: Why We Sleep: Unlocking the Power of Sleep and Dreams
walker-2017.note: Authoritative sleep neuroscience. Hippocampal consolidation, REM and memory integration. Quoted widely in CDP for the sleep-cognition bridge.

barrett-2017.title: How Emotions Are Made: The Secret Life of the Brain
barrett-2017.note: Theory of constructed emotion. Useful for re-framing emotional states as predictions the brain makes, not fixed objects.

clark-2016.title: Surfing Uncertainty: Prediction, Action, and the Embodied Mind
clark-2016.note: Predictive processing framework: brain as prediction machine. Theoretical bridge for symbol-as-prior.

craig-2002.title: How do you feel? Interoception: the sense of the physiological condition of the body
craig-2002.note: Foundational interoception review. Key reference for the body-as-compass premise in CDP.

porges-2011.title: The Polyvagal Theory: Neurophysiological Foundations of Emotions, Attachment, Communication, and Self-Regulation
porges-2011.note: Polyvagal theory foundational text. Concept of neuroception - non-conscious threat/safety detection - is the contemplative-clinical bridge.

cajochen-2013.title: Evidence that the lunar cycle influences human sleep
cajochen-2013.note: Documented effect of lunar phase on sleep architecture and melatonin in controlled lab conditions. Subtle but measurable; the strongest empirical lunar-sleep evidence.

cajochen-schmidt-2024.title: Circadian cognition and sleep
cajochen-schmidt-2024.note: Current synthesis on circadian regulation of cognition. Used by CDP for the Today's Pacing strip rationale.

usno.title: Astronomical Applications - Moon Phases
usno.note: Authoritative source for moon phase timing. CDP draws lunar data from USNO.

arguelles-1987.title: The Mayan Factor: Path Beyond Technology
arguelles-1987.note: The Dreamspell synthesis. CDP labels this clearly as Argüelles 1987 and distinguishes it from the living K'iche' tzolk'in count. Symbolic-archetypal framework, not empirical claim.

tedlock-1992.title: Time and the Highland Maya
tedlock-1992.note: Ethnographic documentation of the living K'iche' Maya tzolk'in count. Anchors CDP's distinction between Argüelles' Dreamspell and traditional Maya calendrics.

sprajc-2023.title: Origins of Mesoamerican astronomy and calendar: Evidence from the Olmec and Maya region
sprajc-2023.note: Peer-reviewed Science Advances paper anchoring the archaeoastronomical literature on Maya calendar tradition. Authoritative.

aldana-2022.title: The Maya Calendar Correlation Problem
aldana-2022.journal: Encyclopedia of the History of Science
aldana-2022.note: Current scholarship on the Maya calendar correlation problem. Useful when the Science voice acknowledges genuine empirical complexity in calendar dating.

aveni-2001.title: Skywatchers: A Revised and Updated Version of Skywatchers of Ancient Mexico
aveni-2001.note: The authoritative reference on Mesoamerican archaeoastronomy. Cited for the distinction between ancient Maya astronomical knowledge and modern Dreamspell synthesis.

bricker-bricker-2011.title: Astronomy in the Maya Codices
bricker-bricker-2011.note: Primary-source scholarship on Maya codex astronomy. Empirical depth for the archaeoastronomical layer.

law-of-time.title: Law of Time Foundation
law-of-time.publisher: Foundation for the Law of Time
law-of-time.note: Dreamspell practitioner authority. CDP cites for the StarRoot/Foundation Kin anchor reference.

hill-2019.title: Period Power: Harness Your Hormones and Get Your Cycle Working For You
hill-2019.note: Contemporary practitioner reference on cycle awareness and the four-phase strategy. Hill is acupuncturist + women's health practitioner. Used for the four-seasons framing labelled clearly as practitioner literature, not empirical hypothesis.

pope-wurlitzer-2017.title: Wild Power: Discover the Magic of Your Menstrual Cycle and Awaken the Feminine Path to Power
pope-wurlitzer-2017.note: Red School four-phase teaching. Practitioner-symbolic framework. The original deep-dive on inner-seasons as a symbolic-developmental container.

jang-2025.title: Menstrual cycle effects on cognitive performance: A meta-analysis
jang-2025.note: Sceptical counterweight on a SPECIFIC and BOUNDED claim. 102 studies, 3,943 participants, 730 comparisons. No systematic robust evidence for cycle effects on OBJECTIVELY-MEASURED cognitive performance tasks: attention, executive function, memory, spatial reasoning, verbal ability. Did NOT test mood, sleep architecture, HPA reactivity, pain perception, autonomic state, social cognition, or subjective experience - those are documented to vary by phase via separate literatures (Lange 2024 mood, Baker & Lee 2023 sleep, Klusmann 2023 HPA, Riley 1999 pain). CDP cites Jang 2025 only on the bounded cognitive-performance claim. The wider claim that 'the cycle modulates the systems within which cognition operates' remains well-supported.

lange-2024.title: Emotion regulation in Premenstrual Dysphoric Disorder and Premenstrual Syndrome: a systematic review
lange-2024.note: Systematic review of emotion regulation across the cycle in PMDD/PMS populations. PRISMA methodology. Phase-specific ER impairments documented in clinical populations - clinical mood/ER effects are real even when general cognition effects are not. Authoritative for the mood-cycle claim with appropriate clinical caveat.

baker-lee-2023.title: The Menstrual Cycle and Sleep
baker-lee-2023.note: Authoritative recent review of cycle-sleep relationship. Subtle but documented changes: increased N2, reduced REM in luteal, increased sleep spindles, raised nocturnal body temperature. Sleep efficiency and onset stable across cycle. Real but modest effect sizes - appropriate for Science voice that promises empirical accuracy.

bower-kuhlman-2023.title: Psychoneuroimmunology: An Introduction to Immune-to-Brain Communication and Its Implications for Clinical Psychology
bower-kuhlman-2023.note: Current PNI synthesis. Annual Review-class authority. Includes HPG-axis interactions with immune signaling.

mengelkoch-2023.title: Multi-omics approaches in psychoneuroimmunology
mengelkoch-2023.note: Multi-omics PNI synthesis. Connects cycle-related immune variation to broader systems biology.

carlson-1985.title: A double-blind test of astrology
carlson-1985.note: Classic sceptical study. Double-blind test in Nature finding astrologers unable to match natal charts to personality profiles above chance. Required citation in Science voice when discussing astrological framing. Establishes that symbolic frameworks are not empirically predictive in the strong sense.

hartmann-2006.title: The relationship between date of birth and individual differences in personality and general intelligence: A large-scale study
hartmann-2006.note: Large-scale study finding no relationship between birth date and personality/intelligence. Cited alongside Carlson 1985 for the sceptical counterweight on natal astrology.

tarnas-2006.title: Cosmos and Psyche: Intimations of a New World View
tarnas-2006.note: Archetypal-cosmological framing of astrology. Tarnas reframes astrology as symbolic rather than predictive. Useful for the Tradition voice.

greene-1976.title: Saturn: A New Look at an Old Devil
greene-1976.note: Foundational psychological astrology. Practitioner depth for the Saturn/developmental layer in the Tradition voice.

siegel-2020.title: The Developing Mind: How Relationships and the Brain Interact to Shape Who We Are
siegel-2020.note: Interpersonal neurobiology synthesis. Bridges relational, contemplative, and neuroscientific frames.

van-dam-2018.title: Mind the Hype: A Critical Evaluation and Prescriptive Agenda for Research on Mindfulness and Meditation
van-dam-2018.note: Sceptical critique of mindfulness overclaiming. Required citation when the Science voice discusses contemplative practice to avoid wellness-industry overclaim.

lee-pittler-ernst-2008.title: Effects of reiki in clinical practice: a systematic review of randomised clinical trials
lee-pittler-ernst-2008.note: The strongest sceptical systematic review of Reiki. Conclusion: evidence insufficient to support efficacy. Included in CDP bibliography precisely because intellectual honesty requires the sceptical literature alongside the supportive.

klusmann-2023.title: Menstrual cycle-related changes in HPA axis reactivity to acute psychosocial and physiological stressors: A systematic review and meta-analysis of longitudinal studies
klusmann-2023.note: Systematic review and meta-analysis (PRISMA) of HPA axis reactivity across the cycle. Small but significant effect: higher cortisol reactivity in luteal vs follicular phase. This is exactly the kind of system-level effect that Jang 2025 did NOT test. Cycling people are more reactive to acute psychosocial stress in the luteal phase. Real, peer-reviewed, current.

klusmann-2022-basal.title: HPA axis activity across the menstrual cycle - a systematic review and meta-analysis of longitudinal studies
klusmann-2022-basal.note: 121 longitudinal studies, 2,641 participants. Basal cortisol higher in follicular than luteal phase (small effect, dSMC=0.12, p=.004). Pairs with Klusmann 2023 reactivity finding: cycle shapes the HPA axis context, even when cognitive performance on tasks does not change.

riley-1999.title: A meta-analytic review of pain perception across the menstrual cycle
riley-1999.note: Foundational meta-analysis. Pain thresholds vary across cycle: higher thresholds (less pain sensitivity) in follicular phase, lower thresholds (more pain sensitivity) in luteal and premenstrual phases. Effect size approximately d=0.40 across stimulus types. Confirmed by recent work (Morales-Lalaguna 2025). Cycling people are more sensitive to pain and sensory stimulation in the premenstrual phase.

morales-lalaguna-2025.title: The Influence of the Menstrual Cycle on Electrical Thresholds for Sensory and Pain Perception
morales-lalaguna-2025.note: Recent confirmation: SET and PET significantly lower in premenstrual phase across all measurement sites. Sensitivity increases as cycle approaches menstruation. Practical implication: exercise tolerance, rehabilitation loading, pain medication needs all phase-dependent.

hand-2002.title: Planets in Transit: Life Cycles for Living
hand-2002.note: Standard practitioner reference for transit interpretation, second edition 2002, ISBN 9780924608261. Carries the transit layer in the Tradition voice. Symbolic, not predictive in the empirical sense.

brennan-2017.title: Hellenistic Astrology: The Study of Fate and Fortune
brennan-2017.note: Comprehensive modern survey of the Hellenistic tradition, ISBN 9780998588902. Grounds the historical and technical origins of Western astrology for the Tradition voice.

campion-2008.title: A History of Western Astrology, Volume I: The Ancient and Classical Worlds
campion-2008.lineage: Cultural history of astrology, Sophia Centre lineage
campion-2008.note: Scholarly cultural history of Western astrology, Continuum 2008, also published as The Dawn of Astrology. Provides the historical context of the astrology layer, treated as cultural history not empirical claim.

grossman-2023.title: Fundamental challenges and likely refutations of the five basic premises of the polyvagal theory
grossman-2023.note: Sceptical counterweight to polyvagal theory. Evaluates the five stated premises, including respiratory sinus arrhythmia as an index of cardiac vagal tone, and finds each untenable on present evidence. Paired with porges-2011 so the Science voice neither overstates polyvagal claims nor dismisses clinical usefulness. Open access.

parr-pezzulo-friston-2022.title: Active Inference: The Free Energy Principle in Mind, Brain, and Behavior
parr-pezzulo-friston-2022.note: Current comprehensive synthesis of active inference, updating clark-2016 and the free-energy principle. Perception, planning, and action as probabilistic inference that minimises prediction error. Open-access monograph.

stein-2021.title: Looking beyond depression: a meta-analysis of the effect of behavioral activation on depression, anxiety, and activation
stein-2021.note: Current major meta-analysis, 28 trials. Large effect on depression (Hedges g approximately 0.83), smaller effects on anxiety and activation. Supersedes ekers-2014 as the summary citation for action-as-intervention.

uphoff-2020.title: Behavioural activation therapy for depression in adults
uphoff-2020.note: Cochrane systematic review, highest evidence tier. Behavioural activation may be more effective than humanistic therapy and treatment as usual for depression, evidence graded low to moderate certainty.

richards-2016-cobra.title: Cost and outcome of behavioural activation versus cognitive behavioural therapy for depression (COBRA): a randomised, controlled, non-inferiority trial
richards-2016-cobra.note: Large pragmatic UK trial, 440 patients. Behavioural activation delivered by junior mental health workers is non-inferior to CBT delivered by psychotherapists, at lower cost. Supports the claim that orientation toward action is effective and scalable.

galante-2021.title: Mindfulness-based programmes for mental health promotion in adults in non-clinical settings: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials
galante-2021.note: Strongest recent meta-analysis of mindfulness in non-clinical settings. Reduces average psychological distress, but the effect is heterogeneous and unlikely to benefit everyone. Honest baseline for what daily contemplative practice can be claimed to do.

galante-2023.title: Systematic review and individual participant data meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials assessing mindfulness-based programmes for mental health promotion
galante-2023.note: Individual-participant-data follow-up to galante-2021. Group-based teacher-led programmes generally reduce distress among volunteers; individual-level moderators of benefit remain unresolved.

goldberg-2022.title: The empirical status of mindfulness-based interventions: a systematic review of 44 meta-analyses of randomised controlled trials
goldberg-2022.note: Umbrella review of 44 meta-analyses. Best single summary of where the mindfulness evidence is strong (depression, anxiety, smoking, pain) and where it is weaker or uncertain.

gloster-2020.title: The empirical status of acceptance and commitment therapy: a review of meta-analyses
gloster-2020.note: Review of 20 meta-analyses, roughly 12,477 participants. ACT efficacious across anxiety, depression, substance use, pain, and transdiagnostic presentations, generally superior to inactive controls. Closest evidenced therapy to CDP's pairing of present-moment attention with values-based daily action. Open access.

farias-2020.title: Adverse events in meditation practices and meditation-based therapies: a systematic review
farias-2020.note: Sceptical counterweight for the contemplative literature. Across 83 studies, pooled prevalence of meditation-related adverse events approximately 8 percent (higher in observational than experimental samples), most commonly anxiety, depression, and cognitive disturbance. Carried so contemplative practice is not presented as uniformly benign.

kuhfuss-2021.title: Somatic experiencing, effectiveness and key factors of a body-oriented trauma therapy: a scoping literature review
kuhfuss-2021.note: First effectiveness review of Somatic Experiencing. Preliminary evidence that it reduces post-traumatic and comorbid symptoms via interoceptive and proprioceptive work, with the caveat that the underlying studies vary considerably in design and quality. Presented at the strength the literature supports. Open access.

nikolova-2021.title: Perturbations in gut microbiota composition in psychiatric disorders: a review and meta-analysis
nikolova-2021.note: Systems-level context only, not a CDP claim surface. Umbrella meta-analysis of 59 case-control studies: gut-microbiota perturbations in psychiatric populations are largely shared across diagnoses rather than disorder-specific, so no microbiome signature is a clean biomarker. CDP makes no gut-based claim in any reading; carried only for the systems framing of the PNI bridge. Open access.

seneca-ep13.title: Epistulae Morales ad Lucilium, Letter 13 (On Groundless Fears)
seneca-ep13.edition: Gummere (trans.), Loeb Classical Library 75, Harvard University Press, 1917; Latin Reynolds, Oxford Classical Texts, 1965
seneca-ep13.note: Symbolic. We suffer more often in imagination than in reality, and the mind torments itself with things that may never come. The tradition voice reaches here for fear and anticipatory worry.

epictetus-enchiridion.title: Encheiridion (The Handbook), chapter 5
epictetus-enchiridion.edition: Oldfather (trans.), Loeb Classical Library 218, Harvard University Press, 1928
epictetus-enchiridion.note: Symbolic. People are disturbed not by events but by their judgements about events. The line the everyday voice reaches for when Seneca runs long.

marcus-meditations.title: Meditations, 8.47
marcus-meditations.edition: Haines (trans.), Loeb Classical Library 58, Harvard University Press, 1916; scholarly text Farquharson, Clarendon Press, 1944
marcus-meditations.note: Symbolic. If you are distressed by something outside you, the pain is your estimate of the thing, and the estimate can be revoked. The same principle written by an emperor to himself.

cicero-tusculans.title: Tusculanae Disputationes, Books III and IV
cicero-tusculans.edition: King (trans.), Loeb Classical Library 141, Harvard University Press, 1927
cicero-tusculans.note: Symbolic. Distress treated as a fresh opinion of a present evil, an assent the mind gives to a mistaken impression. The theory behind the aphorisms.

diogenes-laertius-7.title: Lives of Eminent Philosophers, Book VII (Zeno and the Stoics)
diogenes-laertius-7.edition: Hicks (trans.), Loeb Classical Library 185, Harvard University Press, 1925
diogenes-laertius-7.note: Symbolic. The principal surviving account of early Stoic doctrine, including the passions as excessive impulses founded on mistaken judgement. The historical root of the appraisal model.

ellis-1962.title: Reason and Emotion in Psychotherapy
ellis-1962.note: Lineage. The founding text of Rational Emotive Behaviour Therapy. Ellis names Epictetus as the origin of his central principle. Efficacy evidence for cognitive therapy is a separate, larger literature cited on its own terms.

robertson-2010.title: The Philosophy of Cognitive Behavioural Therapy: Stoic Philosophy as Rational and Cognitive Psychotherapy
robertson-2010.note: Lineage. The scholarly history of the line from Stoic appraisal to cognitive behavioural therapy. The reason CDP can say this bridge is recovered rather than invented. Distinct from Robertson 2019.

lieberman-2007.title: Putting feelings into words: affect labeling disrupts amygdala activity in response to affective stimuli
lieberman-2007.note: Empirical and bounded. Naming an emotion reduced amygdala response to negative images and raised right ventrolateral prefrontal activity. A single fMRI study with a small sample; affect-labeling effects are real but modest and context-dependent. Carried as a suggestive convergence with the Stoic move, not as proof.

meeus-1998.title: Astronomical Algorithms (2nd ed)
meeus-1998.lineage: Astronomical foundation layer (currently absent from formal bibliography)
meeus-1998.note: Application. Definitive reference for ephemeris calculation. The standard manual used by astronomical software developers worldwide.

authors: Folkner, W.M., Williams, J.G., Boggs, D.H., Park, R.S.
folkner-2014.title: The Planetary and Lunar Ephemerides DE430 and DE431
folkner-2014.lineage: Astronomical foundation layer (currently absent from formal bibliography)
folkner-2014.note: Application. The technical specification for the JPL ephemeris that Swiss Ephemeris is built on. Citation for any 'JPL-derived' claim CDP makes.

urban-2013.title: Explanatory Supplement to the Astronomical Almanac (3rd ed)
urban-2013.source: University Science Books, Mill Valley CA
urban-2013.lineage: Astronomical foundation layer (currently absent from formal bibliography)
urban-2013.note: Application. The USNO and HM Nautical Almanac Office joint reference. Source of authority for moon phase definitions and date conventions used in CDP.

authors: Casiraghi, L., Spiousas, I., Dunster, G.P., McGlothlen, K., Fernández-Duque, E., Valeggia, C.
casiraghi-2021.title: Moonstruck sleep: Synchronization of human sleep with the moon cycle under field conditions
casiraghi-2021.lineage: Astronomical foundation layer (currently absent from formal bibliography)
casiraghi-2021.note: Application. Argentinian field study showing real lunar synchrony in sleep timing absent artificial light. The cleanest current peer-reviewed evidence for a moon-cycle effect on humans.

authors: Helfrich-Förster, C., Monecke, S., Spiousas, I., Hovestadt, T., Mitesser, O.
helfrich_forster-2021.title: Women temporarily synchronize their menstrual cycles with the luminance and gravimetric cycles of the Moon
helfrich_forster-2021.lineage: Astronomical foundation layer (currently absent from formal bibliography)
helfrich_forster-2021.note: Application. Companion paper to Casiraghi et al. 2021, on menstrual cycle and lunar synchrony. Both should be cited together for the lunar effects layer.

aldana-2007.title: The Apotheosis of Janaab' Pakal: Science, History, and Religion at Classic Maya Palenque
aldana-2007.lineage: Maya calendrics additions (extending the existing core)
aldana-2007.note: Application. Aldana's full-length intellectual history of Maya astronomical thought. Oracle-tier depth source for Venus tables and Classic-period calendrics.

coe-1992.title: Breaking the Maya Code
coe-1992.lineage: Maya calendrics additions (extending the existing core)
coe-1992.note: Application. The decipherment story. Useful background for Oracle-tier readers who want the intellectual history of how Maya calendrics became readable.

vail-2009.title: The Madrid Codex: New Approaches to Understanding an Ancient Maya Manuscript
vail-2009.lineage: Maya calendrics additions (extending the existing core)
vail-2009.note: Application. Companion volume to Bricker and Bricker 2011. Specifically on the Madrid Codex which contains key 260-day count tables.

hoopes-2011.title: New Age Sympathies and Scholarly Complicities: The History and Promotion of 2012 Mythology
hoopes-2011.source: Archaeoastronomy: The Journal of Astronomy in Culture, 24, 183-205
hoopes-2011.lineage: Maya calendrics additions (extending the existing core)
hoopes-2011.note: Application. Critical analysis essential to maintain scholarly distance from 2012 mythologies. Strengthens the credibility of CDP's transparency notes.

arguelles-1996.title: Dreamspell: The Journey of Timeship Earth 2013
arguelles-1996.source: Foundation for the Law of Time, Ashland OR
arguelles-1996.lineage: Dreamspell and Law of Time (modern 1987 system; currently uncited)
arguelles-1996.note: Application. The codification of the Dreamspell system including the 13-tone, 20-glyph Kin matrix CDP uses. Always cite alongside the academic clarification that this is not ancient Maya doctrine.

sitler-2006.title: The 2012 Phenomenon: New Age Appropriation of an Ancient Mayan Calendar
sitler-2006.source: Nova Religio: The Journal of Alternative and Emergent Religions, 9(3), 24-38
sitler-2006.lineage: Dreamspell and Law of Time (modern 1987 system; currently uncited)
sitler-2006.note: Application. Peer-reviewed scholarly analysis of how Argüelles' framework relates to actual Maya tradition. The strongest single citation for the framework-transparency claim CDP makes.

greene-1984.title: The Astrology of Fate
greene-1984.lineage: Western astrology: historical, interpretive and empirical
greene-1984.note: Application. Greene's full statement on the relationship between archetypal patterns and lived experience. Oracle-tier interpretive reference.

brady-1999.title: Predictive Astrology: The Eagle and the Lark
brady-1999.lineage: Western astrology: historical, interpretive and empirical
brady-1999.note: Application. The leading modern text on progressions, eclipses, and saros cycles. Reference for Oracle-tier timing windows.

campion-2009.title: A History of Western Astrology, Volume II: The Medieval and Modern Worlds
campion-2009.lineage: Western astrology: historical, interpretive and empirical
campion-2009.note: Application. Volume II of Campion's history. Together these are the only academic two-volume history of the practice.

barrett-2015.title: Interoceptive predictions in the brain
barrett-2015.note: Application. The neural-architecture paper for the constructed-emotion model. Bridges Craig's interoception work to Barrett's emotion theory.

barrett-2015-2.title: The Psychological Construction of Emotion
barrett-2015-2.note: Application. The edited volume bringing together the leading constructionist researchers. For the broader theoretical context.

craig-2009.title: How do you feel, now? The anterior insula and human awareness
craig-2009.lineage: Interoception: primary sources and consensus statement
craig-2009.note: Application. Craig's update extending the interoceptive model to consciousness and self-awareness.

authors: Khalsa, S.S., Adolphs, R., Cameron, O.G., Critchley, H.D., Davenport, P.W., Feinstein, J.S., et al
khalsa-2018.title: Interoception and Mental Health: A Roadmap
khalsa-2018.source: Biological Psychiatry: Cognitive Neuroscience and Neuroimaging, 3(6), 501-513
khalsa-2018.lineage: Interoception: primary sources and consensus statement
khalsa-2018.note: Application. The 2016 Interoception Summit consensus statement, signed by 24 of the field's leading researchers. The expert-defined definition CDP should cite when explaining interoception.

critchley-2017.title: Interoception and emotion
critchley-2017.lineage: Interoception: primary sources and consensus statement
critchley-2017.note: Application. The cleanest short review linking interoception to emotion. Sussex group; central to the UK interoception research community.

authors: Garfinkel, S.N., Seth, A.K., Barrett, A.B., Suzuki, K.
garfinkel-2015.title: Knowing your own heart: distinguishing interoceptive accuracy from interoceptive awareness
garfinkel-2015.lineage: Interoception: primary sources and consensus statement
garfinkel-2015.note: Application. The methodological paper establishing the accuracy/sensibility/awareness distinction. Required for any precise claim CDP makes about interoceptive practice.

bowlby-1969.title: Attachment and Loss, Vol
bowlby-1969.source: 1: Attachment (2nd ed). Basic Books, New York
bowlby-1969.lineage: Attachment theory: foundational sources for the relationship reading
bowlby-1969.note: Application. The foundational text. Internal working models, the attachment behavioural system, and the biological basis. Required citation.

bowlby-1973.title: Attachment and Loss, Vol
bowlby-1973.source: 2: Separation, Anxiety and Anger. Basic Books, New York
bowlby-1973.lineage: Attachment theory: foundational sources for the relationship reading
bowlby-1973.note: Application. Volume II. The separation responses that ground anxiety in unmet attachment needs.

bowlby-1980.title: Attachment and Loss, Vol
bowlby-1980.source: 3: Loss, Sadness and Depression. Basic Books, New York
bowlby-1980.lineage: Attachment theory: foundational sources for the relationship reading
bowlby-1980.note: Application. Volume III. The grief and loss layer. Useful for shadow-work content involving loss themes.

authors: Ainsworth, M.D.S., Blehar, M.C., Waters, E.
ainsworth-1978.title: Patterns of Attachment: A Psychological Study of the Strange Situation
ainsworth-1978.lineage: Attachment theory: foundational sources for the relationship reading
ainsworth-1978.note: Application. The Strange Situation procedure that operationalised secure, anxious, and avoidant styles. Source for any CDP claim about attachment styles.

main-1985.title: Security in infancy, childhood, and adulthood: A move to the level of representation
main-1985.source: Monographs of the Society for Research in Child Development, 50(1/2), 66-104
main-1985.lineage: Attachment theory: foundational sources for the relationship reading
main-1985.note: Application. The Adult Attachment Interview. Establishes that adult mental representations of attachment predict their children's styles. The bridge from developmental to adult work.

mikulincer-2016.title: Attachment in Adulthood: Structure, Dynamics, and Change (2nd ed)
mikulincer-2016.lineage: Attachment theory: foundational sources for the relationship reading
mikulincer-2016.note: Application. The current standard text on adult attachment. The empirical synthesis CDP should cite for relationship-reading claims at Mystic and Oracle tiers.

authors: Fonagy, P., Gergely, G., Jurist, E.L.
fonagy-2002.title: Affect Regulation, Mentalization, and the Development of the Self
fonagy-2002.lineage: Attachment theory: foundational sources for the relationship reading
fonagy-2002.note: Application. The mentalisation framework which extends attachment theory into adult emotional regulation. Particularly useful for shadow-work explanations.

siegel-2012.title: Pocket Guide to Interpersonal Neurobiology: An Integrative Handbook of the Mind
siegel-2012.lineage: Attachment theory: foundational sources for the relationship reading
siegel-2012.note: Application. The accessible companion to The Developing Mind. Useful as a public-tier explanatory source.

porges-2022.title: Polyvagal Theory: A Science of Safety
porges-2022.note: Application. Porges's most recent peer-reviewed clarification of the theory and response to critics. Required citation alongside the 2011 book.

critchley-2013.title: Visceral influences on brain and behavior
critchley-2013.note: Application. The mainstream neuroscience review of body-to-brain pathways. Provides the more conservative scientific framing alongside Porges.

clark-2013.title: Whatever next? Predictive brains, situated agents, and the future of cognitive science
clark-2013.note: Application. The single most-cited statement of predictive processing for general cognitive science readers. Highly cited target article in BBS.

friston-2010.title: The free-energy principle: a unified brain theory?
friston-2010.note: Application. Friston's defining statement of the free-energy principle. The mathematical foundation underneath Clark's framework.

hohwy-2013.title: The Predictive Mind
hohwy-2013.note: Application. The philosophical companion to Clark 2016. Tighter on the metaphysics, especially relevant for the 'frameworks shape what we experience' claim.

seth-2013.title: Interoceptive inference, emotion, and the embodied self
seth-2013.note: Application. The bridge paper between predictive processing and interoception. Explicitly relevant to the daily-reading-as-conceptual-framing argument.

walker-2004.title: Sleep-dependent learning and memory consolidation
walker-2004.note: Application. The peer-reviewed primary source for the hippocampal-consolidation claim. Required scholarly citation.

diekelmann-2010.title: The memory function of sleep
diekelmann-2010.note: Application. The Lübeck group's synthesis of how sleep consolidates declarative and procedural memory. Pairs with Walker.

hebb-1949.title: The Organization of Behavior: A Neuropsychological Theory
hebb-1949.note: Application. The book Volume 4 cites by year. The foundational text of neuroplasticity ('cells that fire together, wire together'). Required citation.

citri-2008.title: Synaptic plasticity: multiple forms, functions, and mechanisms
citri-2008.note: Application. The cleanest mainstream review of synaptic plasticity mechanisms. Stanford group.

authors: Pascual-Leone, A., Amedi, A., Fregni, F.
pascual_leone-2005.title: The plastic human brain cortex
pascual_leone-2005.note: Application. The Annual Review article on adult cortical plasticity. Strongest single citation for the claim that daily practice physically changes brain structure.

jung-1954.title: Alchemical Studies
jung-1954.source: Collected Works, Vol. 13. Princeton University Press, Princeton NJ
jung-1954.lineage: Jungian depth psychology and shadow work
jung-1954.note: Application. The volume Volume 4 cites for the central shadow-work claim. Required primary source.

jung-1959.title: Aion: Researches into the Phenomenology of the Self
jung-1959.source: Collected Works, Vol. 9, Part II. Princeton University Press, Princeton NJ
jung-1959.lineage: Jungian depth psychology and shadow work
jung-1959.note: Application. Jung's most systematic statement on the Self archetype and shadow integration. For Oracle-tier depth content.

jung-1964.title: Man and His Symbols
jung-1964.lineage: Jungian depth psychology and shadow work
jung-1964.note: Application. The accessible Jungian primer. Useful for general readers approaching shadow content for the first time.

stein-1998.title: Jung's Map of the Soul: An Introduction
stein-1998.lineage: Jungian depth psychology and shadow work
stein-1998.note: Application. The cleanest contemporary synthesis of Jungian individuation as a daily practice. Cited by name in Volume 4.

stein-2006.title: The Principle of Individuation: Toward the Development of Human Consciousness
stein-2006.lineage: Jungian depth psychology and shadow work
stein-2006.note: Application. Stein's full statement on individuation. For Oracle-tier depth on the integration framework underlying CDP's evening reflection.

authors: These references answer the question 'why do daily practices grounded in symbolic frameworks work?' from the cultural-anthropology side. They are not strictly required for any CDP feature, but they make CDP defensible to a reader trained in religious studies or cultural anthropology, which strengthens institutional credibility. van Gennep, A
these_references_answer_the_question_why.title: The Rites of Passage (M.B
these_references_answer_the_question_why.source: Vizedom & G.L. Caffee, Trans.). University of Chicago Press, Chicago
these_references_answer_the_question_why.note: Application. The foundational text on transition rituals across cultures. The 'three phases' structure (separation, liminality, reintegration) maps onto CDP's morning/midday/evening framework.

turner-1969.title: The Ritual Process: Structure and Anti-Structure
turner-1969.note: Application. The extension of van Gennep's framework. Liminality as a specific category, central to any analysis of daily ritual.

eliade-1959.title: The Sacred and the Profane: The Nature of Religion (W.R
eliade-1959.source: Trask, Trans.). Harcourt Brace, New York
eliade-1959.note: Application. The most-cited single text in comparative religion on the structure of sacred time. Foundational for any claim that 'a day has a quality'.

bell-1992.title: Ritual Theory, Ritual Practice
bell-1992.note: Application. The leading modern synthesis of ritual theory. Cite for academic claims about the cognitive functions of ritual practice.

geertz-1973.title: The Interpretation of Cultures
geertz-1973.note: Application. Geertz's 'thick description' framework, particularly the essays on religion as a cultural system. Foundational anthropology.

park-2010.title: Making sense of the meaning literature: An integrative review of meaning making and its effects on adjustment to stressful life events
park-2010.note: Application. The most-cited synthesis of meaning-making research in clinical psychology. Required citation for any claim CDP makes about reading-as-sense-making.

mcadams-2001.title: The psychology of life stories
mcadams-2001.note: Application. The narrative-identity framework. Provides scientific grounding for the 'your cosmic signature' framing CDP uses for Birth Kin and Life Path.

authors: Steger, M.F., Frazier, P., Oishi, S.
steger-2006.title: The Meaning in Life Questionnaire: Assessing the presence of and search for meaning in life
steger-2006.note: Application. The validated measure of meaning in life. Useful if CDP ever runs internal user studies on whether the platform increases meaning.

pennebaker-1997.title: Writing about emotional experiences as a therapeutic process
pennebaker-1997.note: Application. Pennebaker's most-cited synthesis of his expressive-writing paradigm. The original empirical foundation for journaling-as-intervention claims.

smyth-1998.title: Written emotional expression: Effect sizes, outcome types, and moderating variables
smyth-1998.source: Journal of Consulting and Clinical Psychology, 66(1), 174-184
smyth-1998.note: Application. The first major meta-analysis. Effect size approximately d = 0.47 across 13 studies on physical and psychological health outcomes.

frattaroli-2006.title: Experimental disclosure and its moderators: A meta-analysis
frattaroli-2006.note: Application. The updated meta-analysis (146 studies). The strongest single statistical citation for CDP's daily-reflection mechanism.

sloan-2018.title: Written Exposure Therapy for PTSD: A Brief Treatment Approach for Mental Health Professionals
sloan-2018.note: Application. The clinical extension to PTSD. Useful for the credibility argument that structured reflective writing is now a recognised therapeutic modality.

kaptchuk-2015.title: Placebo effects in medicine
kaptchuk-2015.note: Application. Kaptchuk's NEJM perspective. The cleanest single citation for the position that expectation effects are real and clinically significant.

benedetti-2014.title: Placebo effects: from the neurobiological paradigm to translational implications
benedetti-2014.note: Application. Benedetti's Turin group has produced the most rigorous placebo-mechanism research. This Neuron review is the field synthesis.

wager-2015.title: The neuroscience of placebo effects: connecting context, learning and health
wager-2015.note: Application. The current standard reference on placebo neuroscience. Wager group at Dartmouth. Required for any rigorous discussion of expectation mechanisms.

sosis-2003.title: Signaling, solidarity, and the sacred: The evolution of religious behavior
sosis-2003.lineage: Sociology of belief and ritual practice
sosis-2003.note: Application. The leading evolutionary-anthropology framework on why ritual practice evolved. Useful for explaining why structured daily practice produces measurable wellbeing benefits.

norenzayan-2013.title: Big Gods: How Religion Transformed Cooperation and Conflict
norenzayan-2013.lineage: Sociology of belief and ritual practice
norenzayan-2013.note: Application. Norenzayan's synthesis of how shared symbolic frameworks coordinate behaviour at scale. Background for the cultural argument behind CDP.

lindeman-2007.title: Superstitious, magical, and paranormal beliefs: An integrative model
lindeman-2007.lineage: Sociology of belief and ritual practice
lindeman-2007.note: Application. The mainstream individual-differences research on belief in symbolic frameworks. Useful background for understanding CDP's user psychology.

roenneberg-2003.title: Life between clocks: Daily temporal patterns of human chronotypes
roenneberg-2003.note: Application. The Munich group's foundational chronotype work, source of the Munich Chronotype Questionnaire (MCTQ).

roenneberg-2016.title: The circadian clock and human health
roenneberg-2016.note: Application. The standard short review of circadian misalignment and health. Pairs cleanly with Walker 2020 and Cajochen and Schmidt 2024 already in your list.

mehrhof-2024.title: A common alteration in effort-based decision-making in apathy, anhedonia, and late circadian rhythm
mehrhof-2024.note: Application. Cambridge group (MRC Cognition and Brain Sciences Unit). The paper Volume 4 cites by name. Demonstrates time-of-day synchrony effects on motivated cognition. Required for the morning/midday/evening framing.

raison-1999.title: The moon and madness reconsidered
raison-1999.note: Application. The systematic critique of the 'lunacy' literature. Cite alongside the supportive Cajochen 2013 work for honest framing of where lunar effects are well-supported and where they are not.

wehr-2018.title: Bipolar mood cycles and lunar tidal cycles
wehr-2018.note: Application. NIH (Wehr was Chief of the Section on Biological Rhythms). Documents lunar synchrony in rapid-cycling bipolar disorder under controlled conditions. Among the strongest single peer-reviewed lunar-effects findings.

dean-1996.title: Astrology
dean-1996.source: In G. Stein (Ed.), The Encyclopedia of the Paranormal (pp. 47-99). Prometheus Books
dean-1996.lineage: Astrology empirical research: additional sceptical and methodological references
dean-1996.note: Application. The most thorough sceptical review of astrology research up to 1996. Companion to Carlson 1985 for the empirical balance.

bauer-1997.title: Belief in astrology: A social-psychological analysis
bauer-1997.lineage: Astrology empirical research: additional sceptical and methodological references
bauer-1997.note: Application. The peer-reviewed sociological study of astrology belief in the UK. Useful for understanding the user base CDP serves.

le_grice-2010.title: The Archetypal Cosmos: Rediscovering the Gods in Myth, Science and Astrology
le_grice-2010.note: Application. Le Grice extends Tarnas's archetypal cosmology with explicit attention to scientific worldview. The strongest single text for CDP's 'two telescopes' philosophical argument.

bishop-2008.title: The Archaic: The Past in the Present
bishop-2008.note: Application. Glasgow philosopher; on how ancient symbolic systems remain operative in modern psychology. Background reading for the convergence claim.

combs-1990.title: Synchronicity: Through the Eyes of Science, Myth, and the Trickster
combs-1990.note: Application. The cleanest accessible synthesis of synchronicity research that takes both Jungian and scientific perspectives seriously.

broadbent-1958.title: Perception and Communication
broadbent-1958.note: Application. The foundational filter model of attention. The starting reference for any claim about selective filtering of incoming stimuli. The 11 million bits per second to 50 conscious bits per second contrast traces back to this lineage.

treisman-1980.title: A feature-integration theory of attention
treisman-1980.note: Application. The most-cited paper in the attention literature. Establishes that attention binds features into coherent percepts. Reference for the Gestalt principle that perception requires active assembly, not passive reception.

posner-1990.title: The attention system of the human brain
posner-1990.note: Application. The three-network model of attention (alerting, orienting, executive control). The neural substrate behind the Compass's attention-cultivation claim. Pairs with Petersen and Posner 2012 update below.

petersen-2012.title: The attention system of the human brain: 20 years after
petersen-2012.note: Application. The two decades-on synthesis. Establishes the consensus view of the three-network model and its developmental trajectory. Required for any developmental claim about attention training.

corbetta-2002.title: Control of goal-directed and stimulus-driven attention in the brain
corbetta-2002.note: Application. The dorsal versus ventral attention systems distinction. Important for the claim that morning intention engages the dorsal goal-directed system, biasing what the ventral stimulus-driven system foregrounds during the day.

menon-2010.title: Saliency, switching, attention and control: A network model of insula function
menon-2010.note: Application. The salience network paper. Anchors the Compass's morning-priming claim in specific neural circuitry: anterior insula and anterior cingulate as the switching mechanism between default mode and central executive. Pairs with Seeley et al. 2007.

authors: Seeley, W.W., Menon, V., Schatzberg, A.F., Keller, J., Glover, G.H., Kenna, H., Reiss, A.L.
seeley-2007.title: Dissociable intrinsic connectivity networks for salience processing and executive control
seeley-2007.note: Application. The original mapping of the salience network as distinct from the central executive network. Cite alongside Menon and Uddin 2010 for any salience-network claim.

wertheimer-1923.title: Untersuchungen zur Lehre von der Gestalt II [Laws of organisation in perceptual forms]
wertheimer-1923.lineage: Gestalt psychology: perception and present-moment field
wertheimer-1923.note: Application. The foundational paper of Gestalt psychology. Establishes the laws of perceptual organisation (proximity, similarity, continuity, closure, common fate). Cite for the principle that the brain perceives wholes, not assemblages.

kohler-1947.title: Gestalt Psychology: An Introduction to New Concepts in Modern Psychology
kohler-1947.lineage: Gestalt psychology: perception and present-moment field
kohler-1947.note: Application. Köhler's mature statement of the Gestalt position, written for a general readership. The cleanest single reference for Gestalt principles outside the technical journal literature.

koffka-1935.title: Principles of Gestalt Psychology
koffka-1935.lineage: Gestalt psychology: perception and present-moment field
koffka-1935.note: Application. The most comprehensive Gestalt treatise. Definitive on the figure-ground distinction that grounds the Reading's convergence-card-on-top architecture.

perls-1951.title: Gestalt Therapy: Excitement and Growth in the Human Personality
perls-1951.lineage: Gestalt psychology: perception and present-moment field
perls-1951.note: Application. The foundational text of Gestalt therapy. Establishes the present-moment field as the operative therapeutic ground. Cite for any reading that surfaces what is alive in the user's now rather than what is past or anticipated.

polster-1973.title: Gestalt Therapy Integrated: Contours of Theory and Practice
polster-1973.lineage: Gestalt psychology: perception and present-moment field
polster-1973.note: Application. The classical practitioner reference for Gestalt therapy as practised. Important for the present-moment focus that distinguishes the Compass from a journal-prompt app.

yontef-1993.title: Awareness, Dialogue, and Process: Essays on Gestalt Therapy
yontef-1993.lineage: Gestalt psychology: perception and present-moment field
yontef-1993.note: Application. The most theoretically rigorous modern Gestalt-therapy reference. Useful for the depth-psychological claim that the Compass surfaces awareness of what is, not directives about what should be.

perls-1992.title: Living at the Boundary: Collected Works of Laura Perls
perls-1992.lineage: Gestalt psychology: perception and present-moment field
perls-1992.note: Application. Laura Perls co-founded Gestalt therapy with Fritz Perls and Paul Goodman; this collection captures her distinct contribution, particularly on the body-grounded present-moment field that the standard Perls/Hefferline/Goodman 1951 text understates. The Polster lineage already cited descends from the New York Institute Laura Perls established. This bibliography draws on training with Laura Perls directly.

authors: Beck, A.T., Rush, A.J., Shaw, B.F.
beck-1979.title: Cognitive Therapy of Depression
beck-1979.lineage: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy: cognitive change as practice
beck-1979.note: Application. The foundational CBT manual. Required citation for any cognitive-restructuring claim. Beck's breakthrough was establishing that distorted thoughts maintain depression, and that examining them reduces it.

beck-2011.title: Cognitive Behavior Therapy: Basics and Beyond (2nd ed)
beck-2011.lineage: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy: cognitive change as practice
beck-2011.note: Application. The current practitioner standard, written by Beck's daughter Judith. The reference clinicians use for CBT structure and technique. Cite for current-best-practice claims.

authors: Hofmann, S.G., Asnaani, A., Vonk, I.J., Sawyer, A.T.
hofmann-2012.title: The efficacy of cognitive behavioral therapy: A review of meta-analyses
hofmann-2012.lineage: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy: cognitive change as practice
hofmann-2012.note: Application. The umbrella meta-analysis of meta-analyses. CBT is among the most empirically supported psychotherapies for a wide range of conditions. The single citation for the CBT-is-evidence-based claim.

clark-2010.title: Cognitive Therapy of Anxiety Disorders: Science and Practice
clark-2010.lineage: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy: cognitive change as practice
clark-2010.note: Application. The standard reference for CBT as applied to anxiety. Useful where the Oracle is responding to scenario-looping, anticipatory anxiety, or worry, all of which the anxiety-CBT literature has substantial expertise on.

young-2003.title: Schema Therapy: A Practitioner's Guide
young-2003.lineage: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy: cognitive change as practice
young-2003.note: Application. Young's schema therapy extends CBT into the deeper schema-level work that the Reading surface is positioned to do. Cite for any reading that surfaces a recurring underlying pattern across multiple intentions over time.

linehan-1993.title: Cognitive-Behavioral Treatment of Borderline Personality Disorder
linehan-1993.lineage: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy: cognitive change as practice
linehan-1993.note: Application. The foundational DBT text. Linehan's integration of CBT with mindfulness and acceptance is conceptually adjacent to CDP's integration of cognitive framing with contemplative practice. Background reference for the multi-modal change literature.

authors: Cuijpers, P., Berking, M., Andersson, G., Quigley, L., Kleiboer, A.
cuijpers-2013.title: A meta-analysis of cognitive-behavioural therapy for adult depression, alone and in comparison with other treatments
cuijpers-2013.lineage: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy: cognitive change as practice
cuijpers-2013.note: Application. The most rigorous meta-analysis of CBT for depression. Cuijpers' Amsterdam group is the leading meta-analytic team in psychotherapy research. Cite for any efficacy claim about CBT.

authors: Bennett-Levy, J., Butler, G., Fennell, M., Hackmann, A., Mueller, M.
bennett_levy-2004.title: Oxford Guide to Behavioural Experiments in Cognitive Therapy
bennett_levy-2004.lineage: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy: cognitive change as practice
bennett_levy-2004.note: Application. The Oxford Cognitive Therapy Centre standard reference for behavioural experimentation in CBT, the technique that distinguishes British clinical CBT from purely cognitive variants. Helen Kennerley is among the contributing authors. This bibliography draws on training with Kennerley directly at the Oxford Centre.

hackmann-2011.title: Oxford Guide to Imagery in Cognitive Therapy
hackmann-2011.lineage: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy: cognitive change as practice
hackmann-2011.note: Application. The standard reference on imagery work within CBT, including the use of mental imagery for emotional processing, schema modification, and rescripting. Particularly relevant for CDP's capacity to surface symbolic and imagistic material in ways that intersect with cognitive change.

greenberger-2015.title: Mind Over Mood: Change How You Feel by Changing the Way You Think (2nd ed)
greenberger-2015.lineage: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy: cognitive change as practice
greenberger-2015.note: Application. The most widely-used CBT self-help workbook by clinicians worldwide, Padesky being one of the most influential CBT teachers since Beck. Useful for understanding the disciplined cognitive-restructuring move at the level of practitioner practice rather than academic theory.

lewinsohn-1974.title: A behavioral approach to depression
lewinsohn-1974.source: In R.J. Friedman & M.M. Katz (Eds.), The Psychology of Depression: Contemporary Theory and Research. Wiley, New York, pp. 157-178
lewinsohn-1974.note: Application. The original behavioural model of depression. Lewinsohn proposed that depression is maintained by a low rate of response-contingent positive reinforcement, and that scheduling activities increases reinforcement and lifts mood. The conceptual ancestor of all subsequent BA work.

authors: Jacobson, N.S., Dobson, K.S., Truax, P.A., Addis, M.E., Koerner, K., Gollan, J.K., Gortner, E.
jacobson-1996.title: A component analysis of cognitive-behavioral treatment for depression
jacobson-1996.source: Journal of Consulting and Clinical Psychology, 64(2), 295-304. doi: 10.1037/0022-006X.64.2.295
jacobson-1996.note: Application. The breakthrough study. Jacobson's component-analysis design isolated the behavioural-activation element of CBT and showed it produced equivalent outcomes to the full cognitive-plus-behavioural protocol. Action alone, without the cognitive restructuring, was as effective.

martell-2001.title: Depression in Context: Strategies for Guided Action
martell-2001.note: Application. The first manualised statement of Behavioural Activation as a stand-alone intervention. Required reference for current BA practice.

authors: Dimidjian, S., Hollon, S.D., Dobson, K.S., Schmaling, K.B., Kohlenberg, R.J., Addis, M.E., Gallop, R., McGlinchey, J.B., Markley, D.K., Gollan, J.K., Atkins, D.C., Dunner, D.L.
dimidjian-2006.title: Randomized trial of behavioral activation, cognitive therapy, and antidepressant medication in the acute treatment of adults with major depression
dimidjian-2006.source: Journal of Consulting and Clinical Psychology, 74(4), 658-670. doi: 10.1037/0022-006X.74.4.658
dimidjian-2006.note: Application. The definitive RCT. BA was equivalent to antidepressant medication and superior to cognitive therapy for severely depressed patients. The empirical pillar for any clinical claim about action-as-medicine.

cuijpers-2007.title: Behavioral activation treatments of depression: A meta-analysis
cuijpers-2007.note: Application. The current meta-analytic synthesis. BA produces large effect sizes for depression, comparable to or exceeding CBT and antidepressants in head-to-head comparisons. The summary citation for evidence-based BA.

authors: Ekers, D., Webster, L., Van Straten, A., Cuijpers, P., Richards, D.
ekers-2014.title: Behavioural activation for depression: An update of meta-analysis of effectiveness and sub group analysis
ekers-2014.note: Application. The most current major meta-analysis. Confirms BA is effective and as good as CBT for depression. Cite alongside Cuijpers et al. 2007 for current consensus.

kabat_zinn-1990.title: Full Catastrophe Living: Using the Wisdom of Your Body and Mind to Face Stress, Pain, and Illness
kabat_zinn-1990.lineage: Mindfulness, MBSR, MBCT, ACT: attention as cultivated practice
kabat_zinn-1990.note: Application. The foundational MBSR text. Kabat-Zinn's breakthrough in operationalising vipassana practice for clinical contexts. The single most important reference for the modern mindfulness movement.

kabat_zinn-2003.title: Mindfulness-based interventions in context: Past, present, and future
kabat_zinn-2003.source: Clinical Psychology: Science and Practice, 10(2), 144-156. doi: 10.1093/clipsy.bpg016
kabat_zinn-2003.lineage: Mindfulness, MBSR, MBCT, ACT: attention as cultivated practice
kabat_zinn-2003.note: Application. Kabat-Zinn's most-cited definitional paper. Establishes mindfulness as paying attention in a particular way: on purpose, in the present moment, non-judgmentally. The standard definition citation.

segal-2013.title: Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy for Depression (2nd ed)
segal-2013.lineage: Mindfulness, MBSR, MBCT, ACT: attention as cultivated practice
segal-2013.note: Application. The current MBCT manual. Required for any claim about mindfulness as a depression-relapse-prevention intervention.

authors: Teasdale, J.D., Segal, Z.V., Williams, J.M.G., Ridgeway, V.A., Soulsby, J.M.
teasdale-2000.title: Prevention of relapse/recurrence in major depression by mindfulness-based cognitive therapy
teasdale-2000.source: Journal of Consulting and Clinical Psychology, 68(4), 615-623. doi: 10.1037/0022-006X.68.4.615
teasdale-2000.lineage: Mindfulness, MBSR, MBCT, ACT: attention as cultivated practice
teasdale-2000.note: Application. The breakthrough RCT showing MBCT reduces depressive relapse in patients with three or more prior episodes by approximately fifty percent compared to treatment as usual. The empirical pillar for the clinical mindfulness literature.

hayes-2012.title: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy: The Process and Practice of Mindful Change (2nd ed)
hayes-2012.lineage: Mindfulness, MBSR, MBCT, ACT: attention as cultivated practice
hayes-2012.note: Application. The foundational ACT text. Hayes' integration of mindfulness with values-based action is the closest existing therapy to CDP's combination of present-moment attention with daily orienting practice.

authors: Goyal, M., Singh, S., Sibinga, E.M.S., Gould, N.F., Rowland-Seymour, A., Sharma, R., Berger, Z., Sleicher, D., Maron, D.D., Shihab, H.M., Ranasinghe, P.D., Linn, S., Saha, S., Bass, E.B.
goyal-2014.title: Meditation programs for psychological stress and well-being: A systematic review and meta-analysis
goyal-2014.lineage: Mindfulness, MBSR, MBCT, ACT: attention as cultivated practice
goyal-2014.note: Application. The Johns Hopkins meta-analysis published in JAMA Internal Medicine. The most rigorous summary of the meditation literature. Establishes moderate evidence for mindfulness in reducing anxiety, depression, and pain. The honest baseline citation for what meditation has and has not been shown to do.

tang-2015.title: The neuroscience of mindfulness meditation
tang-2015.lineage: Mindfulness, MBSR, MBCT, ACT: attention as cultivated practice
tang-2015.note: Application. The Nature Reviews Neuroscience synthesis of meditation's neural effects. Documents changes in attention networks, default mode network, body awareness, emotional regulation, and self-perspective. The single best reference for the brain-changes claim about meditation.

authors: Hölzel, B.K., Carmody, J., Vangel, M., Congleton, C., Yerramsetti, S.M., Gard, T.
holzel-2011.title: Mindfulness practice leads to increases in regional brain gray matter density
holzel-2011.lineage: Mindfulness, MBSR, MBCT, ACT: attention as cultivated practice
holzel-2011.note: Application. The grey-matter-density study. Eight weeks of MBSR produced measurable increases in left hippocampus, posterior cingulate cortex, and other regions. Cite for the structural-plasticity claim about meditation.

levine-1997.title: Waking the Tiger: Healing Trauma
levine-1997.lineage: Somatic and embodied traditions: the body knows
levine-1997.note: Application. The foundational text of Somatic Experiencing. Levine's observation that animals discharge survival energy through trembling and movement, and that humans benefit from completing this process, is the operative principle behind body-based trauma work.

levine-2010.title: In an Unspoken Voice: How the Body Releases Trauma and Restores Goodness
levine-2010.lineage: Somatic and embodied traditions: the body knows
levine-2010.note: Application. Levine's mature statement of the Somatic Experiencing framework. The current standard reference.

van_der_kolk-2014.title: The Body Keeps the Score: Brain, Mind, and Body in the Healing of Trauma
van_der_kolk-2014.lineage: Somatic and embodied traditions: the body knows
van_der_kolk-2014.note: Application. The most widely-read modern synthesis of trauma neuroscience and somatic therapeutic approaches. Required for any reference to body-based trauma work in popular discourse.

damasio-1994.title: Descartes' Error: Emotion, Reason, and the Human Brain
damasio-1994.lineage: Somatic and embodied traditions: the body knows
damasio-1994.note: Application. Damasio's foundational somatic-marker hypothesis. Establishes that emotion is not the enemy of reason but its substrate. The philosophical hinge for any embodied-cognition claim.

damasio-2010.title: Self Comes to Mind: Constructing the Conscious Brain
damasio-2010.lineage: Somatic and embodied traditions: the body knows
damasio-2010.note: Application. Damasio's mature theory of consciousness as built up from body-mapping in the brainstem and insula. Important for the claim that the higher self is rooted in bodily integration, not separate from it.

lakoff-1999.title: Philosophy in the Flesh: The Embodied Mind and Its Challenge to Western Thought
lakoff-1999.lineage: Somatic and embodied traditions: the body knows
lakoff-1999.note: Application. The most thorough philosophical defence of embodied cognition. Argues that abstract reason is metaphorically structured by bodily experience. Background for any claim that contemplative practice integrates mind and body rather than separating them.

white-2008.title: An Introduction to Western Medical Acupuncture
white-2008.lineage: Somatic and embodied traditions: the body knows
white-2008.note: Application. The British Medical Acupuncture Society standard. Western Medical Acupuncture interprets the traditional points through musculoskeletal and neurological mechanisms, providing a defensible bridge between the ancient practice and contemporary pain physiology. This bibliography draws on formal training in Western Medical Acupuncture.

authors: Vickers, A.J., Vertosick, E.A., Lewith, G., MacPherson, H., Foster, N.E., Sherman, K.J., Irnich, D., Witt, C.M.
vickers-2018.title: Acupuncture for chronic pain: Update of an individual patient data meta-analysis
vickers-2018.lineage: Somatic and embodied traditions: the body knows
vickers-2018.note: Application. The definitive individual-patient-data meta-analysis. Establishes that acupuncture produces clinically meaningful effects for chronic musculoskeletal, headache, and osteoarthritis pain that exceed placebo. The most rigorous current evidence summary.

langevin-2002.title: Relationship of acupuncture points and meridians to connective tissue planes
langevin-2002.lineage: Somatic and embodied traditions: the body knows
langevin-2002.note: Application. The Vermont mechanism paper. Maps acupuncture points to connective-tissue plane intersections, providing an anatomical substrate that connects traditional point locations to modern fascia research. Background for the somatic-tradition references.

cassar-1999.title: Handbook of Clinical Massage: A Complete Guide for Students and Practitioners (2nd ed)
cassar-1999.lineage: Somatic and embodied traditions: the body knows
cassar-1999.note: Application. Standard reference for clinical massage including acupressure technique. Useful as the practitioner reference for hands-on bodywork integrating multiple traditions of touch.

hadot-1995.title: Philosophy as a Way of Life: Spiritual Exercises from Socrates to Foucault (M
hadot-1995.source: Chase, Trans.; A.I. Davidson, Ed.). Blackwell, Oxford
hadot-1995.lineage: Stoic, Buddhist and contemplative traditions, and modern devotional teachers
hadot-1995.note: Application. Hadot's recovery of ancient philosophy as lived spiritual practice. The philosophical defence of why a daily reflection practice has substantive value, not merely psychological benefit.

robertson-2019.title: How to Think Like a Roman Emperor: The Stoic Philosophy of Marcus Aurelius
robertson-2019.lineage: Stoic, Buddhist and contemplative traditions, and modern devotional teachers
robertson-2019.note: Application. Robertson is a CBT therapist and Stoic-philosophy scholar; this book explicitly bridges Stoic spiritual exercises with contemporary cognitive-behavioural practice. Important for the claim that the Compass is doing what Stoics did, supported now by what CBT has empirically validated.

wallace-2006.title: Contemplative Science: Where Buddhism and Neuroscience Converge
wallace-2006.lineage: Stoic, Buddhist and contemplative traditions, and modern devotional teachers
wallace-2006.note: Application. Wallace was a Buddhist monk and is a contemporary academic. His treatment of contemplative practice as scientifically tractable and philosophically rigorous is foundational for CDP's combination of contemplative depth with empirical grounding.

kornfield-1993.title: A Path with Heart: A Guide Through the Perils and Promises of Spiritual Life
kornfield-1993.lineage: Stoic, Buddhist and contemplative traditions, and modern devotional teachers
kornfield-1993.note: Application. Kornfield's integration of Theravada Buddhist practice with Western psychological insight. The standard reference for contemplative practice as it lands in modern Western lives.

analayo-2003.title: Satipatthana: The Direct Path to Realization
analayo-2003.lineage: Stoic, Buddhist and contemplative traditions, and modern devotional teachers
analayo-2003.note: Application. The most rigorous contemporary scholarly treatment of the foundational Buddhist text on mindfulness practice. Required for any claim about the Pali-canonical roots of modern mindfulness.

sathya_sai_baba-1953.title: Sathya Sai Speaks (Vols 1-42)
sathya_sai_baba-1953.source: Sri Sathya Sai Books and Publications Trust, Prasanthi Nilayam
sathya_sai_baba-1953.lineage: Stoic, Buddhist and contemplative traditions, and modern devotional teachers
sathya_sai_baba-1953.note: Application. The collected discourses of Sathya Sai Baba across nearly six decades. The primary source for the Sai Baba teaching of integrated practice across the four values: Sathya (truth), Dharma (right action), Shanti (peace), Prema (love). Essential for any reading whose user names this lineage.

murphet-1971.title: Sai Baba: Man of Miracles
murphet-1971.lineage: Stoic, Buddhist and contemplative traditions, and modern devotional teachers
murphet-1971.note: Application. Murphet was a former Royal Air Force officer and journalist; his careful biographical work is among the most measured Western accounts of Sai Baba's life and teaching. Useful as a starting reference for users new to the lineage.

kasturi-1968.title: Sathyam Sivam Sundaram: The Life of Bhagavan Sri Sathya Sai Baba (Vols 1-4)
kasturi-1968.source: Sri Sathya Sai Books and Publications Trust, Prasanthi Nilayam
kasturi-1968.lineage: Stoic, Buddhist and contemplative traditions, and modern devotional teachers
kasturi-1968.note: Application. The authorised four-volume biography by Sai Baba's long-time editor. The standard internal reference within the Sai Baba tradition.

babb-1986.title: Redemptive Encounters: Three Modern Styles in the Hindu Tradition
babb-1986.lineage: Stoic, Buddhist and contemplative traditions, and modern devotional teachers
babb-1986.note: Application. Babb's Amherst College anthropological treatment of contemporary Hindu devotional movements, including the Sai Baba movement. The standard scholarly external reference.

srinivas-2008.title: In the Presence of Sai Baba: Body, City, and Memory in a Global Religious Movement
srinivas-2008.lineage: Stoic, Buddhist and contemplative traditions, and modern devotional teachers
srinivas-2008.note: Application. Smriti Srinivas (UC Davis) provides the most thorough scholarly study of the global Sai Baba movement and its embodied practices. Useful for understanding the contemporary devotional context.

srinivas-2010.title: Winged Faith: Rethinking Globalization and Religious Pluralism Through the Sathya Sai Movement
srinivas-2010.lineage: Stoic, Buddhist and contemplative traditions, and modern devotional teachers
srinivas-2010.note: Application. Tulasi Srinivas (Emerson) on the Sai Baba movement's global character and its teachings on religious unity (Sarva Dharma). Important for cross-framework synthesis claims.

seligman-2002.title: Authentic Happiness: Using the New Positive Psychology to Realize Your Potential for Lasting Fulfillment
seligman-2002.note: Application. The first major popular statement of positive psychology. Established the field as a counter-orientation to deficit-focused clinical psychology.

seligman-2011.title: Flourish: A Visionary New Understanding of Happiness and Well-Being
seligman-2011.note: Application. Seligman's mature PERMA model (Positive emotion, Engagement, Relationships, Meaning, Accomplishment). The current reference for the multi-dimensional structure of wellbeing.

csikszentmihalyi-1990.title: Flow: The Psychology of Optimal Experience
csikszentmihalyi-1990.note: Application. The foundational Flow text. Identifies the state of absorbed engagement as the reliable correlate of intrinsic satisfaction. Important for any reading that names engagement as a value.

neff-2003.title: The development and validation of a scale to measure self-compassion
neff-2003.note: Application. The original self-compassion scale paper. Establishes self-compassion as the combination of self-kindness, common humanity, and mindfulness. The empirical foundation for the construct.

neff-2013.title: A pilot study and randomized controlled trial of the mindful self-compassion program
neff-2013.note: Application. The MSC efficacy trial. Demonstrates that self-compassion is trainable and that training reduces depression, anxiety, and stress. Cite for any clinical claim about self-compassion as a learnable practice.

gilbert-2009.title: The Compassionate Mind: A New Approach to Life's Challenges
gilbert-2009.note: Application. Gilbert's Compassion Focused Therapy, developed for clients whose self-criticism blocks standard CBT. The clinical extension of self-compassion research. Important for the Oracle's capacity to meet self-critical patterns with explicit clinical authority.

ryan-2000.title: Self-determination theory and the facilitation of intrinsic motivation, social development, and well-being
ryan-2000.note: Application. The foundational SDT paper. Establishes autonomy, competence, and relatedness as universal psychological needs. The most-cited paper in motivation research. Essential for the design claim that the Compass satisfies SDT needs by its structure, not only its content.

diamond-2013.title: Executive functions
diamond-2013.note: Application. The standard current reference on executive function. Diamond identifies inhibitory control, working memory, and cognitive flexibility as the three core EFs. The foundational citation for any EF claim.

miyake-2012.title: The nature and organization of individual differences in executive functions: Four general conclusions
miyake-2012.source: Current Directions in Psychological Science, 21(1), 8-14. doi: 10.1177/0963721411429458
miyake-2012.note: Application. The unity-and-diversity framework of EF: a common factor underlies all EF tasks, with separable components for working memory updating and shifting. Important for any claim about EF as both a general and a specific capacity.

hofmann-2012-2.title: Executive functions and self-regulation
hofmann-2012-2.note: Application. The explicit linkage of executive function to self-regulation. Establishes that self-regulatory capacity is grounded in working memory and inhibitory control. Cite for any claim about the Compass as cultivation of self-regulation.

diamond-2016.title: Conclusions about interventions, programs, and approaches for improving executive functions that appear justified and those that, despite much hype, do not
diamond-2016.note: Application. Diamond's honest meta-review of what EF interventions have and have not been shown to do. Important for keeping CDP's claims honest about what daily practice can plausibly cultivate.

maharishi_mahesh_yogi-1969.title: Maharishi Mahesh Yogi on the Bhagavad-Gita: A New Translation and Commentary, Chapters 1-6
maharishi_mahesh_yogi-1969.lineage: Transcendental Meditation as a distinct contemplative lineage
maharishi_mahesh_yogi-1969.note: Application. Maharishi's primary published statement of the philosophical framework underlying Transcendental Meditation, presented through commentary on the Bhagavad-Gita. The doctrinal source for the TM tradition.

science-3926.lineage: Transcendental Meditation as a distinct contemplative lineage
science-3926.note: Application. The Science paper that first brought TM into the peer-reviewed scientific literature. Demonstrated reliable physiological signatures (oxygen consumption, skin resistance, EEG) distinct from rest or sleep. The historical inflection point.

authors: Schneider, R.H., Grim, C.E., Rainforth, M.V., Kotchen, T., Nidich, S.I., Gaylord-King, C., Salerno, J.W., Kotchen, J.M.
schneider-2012.title: Stress reduction in the secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease: Randomized, controlled trial of Transcendental Meditation and health education in Blacks
schneider-2012.source: Circulation: Cardiovascular Quality and Outcomes, 5(6), 750-758. doi: 10.1161/CIRCOUTCOMES.112.967406
schneider-2012.lineage: Transcendental Meditation as a distinct contemplative lineage
schneider-2012.note: Application. NIH-funded randomised trial showing TM reduced cardiovascular events by 48 percent in patients with established coronary disease over an average 5.4 year follow-up. Among the strongest single empirical findings for any meditation practice in cardiovascular outcome research.

orme_johnson-2014.title: Effects of the Transcendental Meditation technique on trait anxiety: A meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
orme_johnson-2014.source: Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine, 20(5), 330-341. doi: 10.1089/acm.2013.0204
orme_johnson-2014.lineage: Transcendental Meditation as a distinct contemplative lineage
orme_johnson-2014.note: Application. Meta-analysis of TM trials on trait anxiety. Establishes effect sizes that exceed comparison meditation and relaxation interventions on this specific outcome.

travis-2010.title: Focused attention, open monitoring and automatic self-transcending: Categories to organize meditations from Vedic, Buddhist and Chinese traditions
travis-2010.lineage: Transcendental Meditation as a distinct contemplative lineage
travis-2010.note: Application. Establishes a three-category taxonomy of meditation techniques and places TM in a distinct category (automatic self-transcending) with its own neural signature, separate from focused-attention practices like concentrative meditation and open-monitoring practices like vipassana. Important for the claim that TM is methodologically distinct from MBSR-style practice.

usui-1922.title: Usui Reiki Ryōhō Hikkei [Handbook of the Usui Reiki Healing Method]
usui-1922.source: Originally circulated within the Usui Reiki Ryōhō Gakkai, Tokyo. English-language transmission via Hayashi-Takata lineage
usui-1922.note: Application. The original founder's instruction document for the practice of Reiki. Primary historical source for the Japanese tradition.

furumoto-1992.title: Reiki: The Original Hand Position System
furumoto-1992.note: Application. Phyllis Furumoto, granddaughter of Hawayo Takata and lineage holder of the Usui Shiki Ryōhō tradition until her death in 2019, is the primary modern reference within the lineage. This bibliography draws on training with Furumoto directly.

lubeck-2001.title: The Spirit of Reiki: The Complete Handbook of the Reiki System
lubeck-2001.note: Application. The most thorough Western-practitioner synthesis. Useful for readers seeking the contemporary practitioner standard outside the closed lineage tradition.

authors: vanderVaart, S., Gijsen, V.M.G.J., de Wildt, S.N.
vandervaart-2009.title: A systematic review of the therapeutic effects of Reiki
vandervaart-2009.source: Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine, 15(11), 1157-1169. doi: 10.1089/acm.2009.0036
vandervaart-2009.note: Application. The systematic review. Concludes that the existing trial evidence is suggestive but methodologically limited, with effects most consistent on anxiety, pain, and depression. Honest framing reference.

joyce-2015.title: Reiki for depression and anxiety
joyce-2015.source: Cochrane Database of Systematic Reviews, 4, CD006833. doi: 10.1002/14651858.CD006833.pub2
joyce-2015.note: Application. The Cochrane review. The highest standard of evidence assessment, concludes that high-quality trials are limited and that more rigorous research is needed. Required citation for any honest claim about the current evidence base.

thrane-2014.title: Effect of Reiki therapy on pain and anxiety in adults: An in-depth literature review of randomized trials with effect size calculations
thrane-2014.note: Application. University of Pittsburgh nursing review focused on pain and anxiety outcomes. Documents moderate effect sizes in nursing-led trials, particularly in cancer and post-surgical contexts. The reference behind Reiki's presence in major US integrative-medicine programmes.

demir_dogan-2018.title: The effect of reiki on pain: A meta-analysis
demir_dogan-2018.source: Complementary Therapies in Clinical Practice, 31, 384-387. doi: 10.1016/j.ctcp.2018.02.020
demir_dogan-2018.note: Application. Pain-specific meta-analysis. Identified consistent moderate effects of reiki on pain reduction across heterogeneous clinical populations, with heterogeneity in study quality. The first meta-analysis specifically focused on pain outcomes; informs CDP's claim that touch-with-attention modalities have measurable effects in clinical contexts.

mcmanus-2017.title: Reiki is better than placebo and has broad potential as a complementary health therapy
mcmanus-2017.source: Journal of Evidence-Based Complementary and Alternative Medicine, 22(4), 1051-1057. doi: 10.1177/2156587217728644
mcmanus-2017.note: Application. The most explicit comparative review of reiki versus placebo controls. Reviewed thirteen studies and concluded reiki produced effects beyond placebo on pain, anxiety, and depression. Required citation for any honest claim that the effects of reiki are not entirely accounted for by expectation alone.
