Read alongside Lee, Pittler and Ernst 2008 for both sides of the methodological debate.

authors: Morero, J.A.P., de Souza Pereira, S., Esteves, R.B.
morero-2021.title: Effects of reiki on mental health care: A systematic review
morero-2021.note: Application. The mental-health-focused systematic review. Surveys reiki applications across anxiety, depression, and stress conditions, identifying consistent effects with the methodological caveats common to the field. The reference for CDP's positioning that touch-with-attention practices have measurable effects in mental-health contexts that are not reducible to placebo alone.

authors: Liu, K., Qin, Z., Qin, Y., et al
liu-2025.title: Effects of reiki therapy on quality of life: A meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
liu-2025.note: Application. The most current major meta-analysis. Six hundred and sixty-one RCT participants pooled across studies through September 2024, showing significant enhancement in quality of life post-reiki therapy (SMD = 0.28, 95 percent CI 0.01 to 0.56, p = 0.043), with subgroup analysis indicating greatest effects with eight or more sessions of sixty minutes or longer. The current top-of-evidence reference for any claim about reiki's effects on quality of life. Cite as the lead recent systematic review when current evidence is the issue.

taylan-2026.title: The effect of reiki on pain, anxiety, and hemodynamic parameters during the perioperative period: A meta-analysis study
taylan-2026.source: Perioperative Care and Operating Room Management, 43, 100644. doi: 10.1016/j.pcorm.2026.100644
taylan-2026.note: Application. The most current perioperative reiki meta-analysis. Demonstrates measurable effects on pain, anxiety, and autonomic markers in surgical populations where the comparison conditions are well-defined. Reference for the specific claim that touch-with-attention practices produce measurable autonomic and subjective shifts in highly-controlled clinical conditions.

national_center_for_complementary-2024.title: Reiki: What you need to know
national_center_for_complementary-2024.source: National Institutes of Health, Bethesda MD
national_center_for_complementary-2024.note: Application. The official US government position on reiki. Acknowledges that some studies have found benefits while noting that most clinical research has methodological limitations and that the energy field central to reiki claims has not been demonstrated by physical measurement. The required institutional reference. Including this is part of the discipline of representing the evidence honestly rather than only positively, which is what the Two Telescopes premise demands.

ader-1975.title: Behaviorally conditioned immunosuppression
ader-1975.note: Application. The founding paper of psychoneuroimmunology. Demonstrated that classical conditioning could produce immunosuppression, establishing that the immune system is regulated by the nervous system rather than autonomous. The paper that opened the field.

ader-2007.title: Psychoneuroimmunology (4th ed, 2 vols)
ader-2007.note: Application. The canonical handbook of the field, edited by its founder. The single reference for any claim about the mechanisms by which psychological and behavioural states affect immune function. The 4th edition is the current standard.

segerstrom-2004.title: Psychological stress and the human immune system: A meta-analytic study of 30 years of inquiry
segerstrom-2004.note: Application. The Psychological Bulletin meta-analysis covering three decades of research on psychological stress and immune function. The single most-cited summary of the stress-immunity relationship.

authors: Kiecolt-Glaser, J.K., McGuire, L., Robles, T.F.
kiecolt_glaser-2002.title: Psychoneuroimmunology: Psychological influences on immune function and health
kiecolt_glaser-2002.source: Journal of Consulting and Clinical Psychology, 70(3), 537-547. doi: 10.1037/0022-006X.70.3.537
kiecolt_glaser-2002.note: Application. Kiecolt-Glaser's Ohio State group has produced the most consistent body of work showing psychological influences on wound healing, vaccination response, and inflammatory markers. This review summarises their findings through 2002; their subsequent work continues to be foundational.

sternberg-2001.title: The Balance Within: The Science Connecting Health and Emotions
sternberg-2001.note: Application. Sternberg, formerly chief of NIMH neuroendocrinology and now at the University of Arizona, provides the most accessible synthesis of psychoneuroimmunology for a wider readership without sacrificing rigour. Useful for explaining the field to non-specialists.

authors: Cole, S.W., Hawkley, L.C., Arevalo, J.M., Sung, C.Y., Rose, R.M.
cole-2007.title: Social regulation of gene expression in human leukocytes
cole-2007.note: Application. Cole's UCLA group identified the Conserved Transcriptional Response to Adversity (CTRA), a gene-expression signature that shifts in response to chronic threat and isolation, and reverses with positive social and contemplative interventions. Among the most important psychoneuroimmunology findings of the past two decades.

authors: Pace, T.W.W., Negi, L.T., Adame, D.D., Cole, S.P., Sivilli, T.I., Brown, T.D., Issa, M.J.
pace-2009.title: Effect of compassion meditation on neuroendocrine, innate immune and behavioral responses to psychosocial stress
pace-2009.note: Application. Emory University RCT showing compassion meditation produced measurable reductions in inflammatory and behavioural responses to psychosocial stress. Direct empirical bridge between contemplative practice and immune function.

authors: Cohen, S., Doyle, W.J., Skoner, D.P., Rabin, B.S.
cohen-1997.title: Social ties and susceptibility to the common cold
cohen-1997.note: Application. The classic social-support-and-immunity demonstration. People with more diverse social ties were less susceptible to viral infection after standardised exposure, in a dose-response gradient. The single cleanest empirical finding linking social connection to immune competence.

science-4865.note: Application. The Science paper synthesising the epidemiological evidence that social isolation is a mortality risk factor comparable in magnitude to smoking, hypertension, and obesity. Foundational reference for the claim that relationship matters for health at the level of survival.

the_lancet-8668.note: Application. The Stanford support-group survival study. Patients with metastatic breast cancer who participated in weekly support groups lived approximately twice as long as controls. Among the most-cited and most-debated findings in psychosocial oncology, and a touchstone for the social-support-survival literature.

itam_eliraz-2024.title: The beneficial effects of social support and prosocial behavior on immunity and health: A psychoneuroimmunology perspective
itam_eliraz-2024.source: Brain, Behavior, and Immunity Health, 37, 100758. doi: 10.1016/j.bbih.2024.100758
itam_eliraz-2024.note: Application. The current synthesis specifically on social support, prosocial behaviour, and immune outcomes. Tel Aviv and UCLA collaboration covering loneliness, social support, prosocial behaviour, and their respective immune signatures. The contemporary equivalent of the Cohen, House, Spiegel literature, written for the post-COVID era. Required citation for any claim CDP makes about social connection and physiological wellbeing.

kuhn-2024.title: Looking back to move forward: Research in stress, behavior, and immune function
kuhn-2024.note: Application. The current historical-and-prospective review of PNI as a field. Useful for the contextual claim that PNI's claims have moved from speculative to mechanistically grounded over the past century, and for situating the contemporary mind-body bridge research within an established empirical tradition.

osterwalder-2010.title: Business Model Generation: A Handbook for Visionaries, Game Changers, and Challengers
osterwalder-2010.note: Application. The Business Model Canvas. The primary reference for the nine-block framework that has become the standard tool for articulating how value is created, delivered, and captured. Foundational for any strategic conversation about what an organisation actually does.

authors: Osterwalder, A., Pigneur, Y., Bernarda, G.
osterwalder-2014.title: Value Proposition Design: How to Create Products and Services Customers Want
osterwalder-2014.note: Application. The Value Proposition Canvas, including the customer profile (jobs to be done, pains, gains) and the value map (products and services, pain relievers, gain creators). The single best operational tool for the discipline of meeting users where they actually are. Directly relevant to how the Oracle can support users navigating relational and strategic questions.

christensen-1997.title: The Innovator's Dilemma: When New Technologies Cause Great Firms to Fail
christensen-1997.note: Application. The foundational text on disruptive innovation. Establishes the argument that established organisations fail not by doing things wrong but by listening too closely to existing customers and missing emerging needs. Background for any strategic conversation about positioning.

authors: Christensen, C.M., Hall, T., Dillon, K.
christensen-2016.title: Competing Against Luck: The Story of Innovation and Customer Choice
christensen-2016.note: Application. Christensen's canonical statement of the Jobs-to-be-Done framework. People do not buy products; they hire products to do a job in their lives. The single best reference for the JTBD discipline. Particularly relevant to how the Oracle thinks about what a user is actually asking for when they type a line into the Compass.

ulwick-2016.title: Jobs to be Done: Theory to Practice
ulwick-2016.note: Application. The operational manual for putting JTBD into practice, by the practitioner who originated much of the formalisation underlying Christensen's framework. The methodology reference for moving from JTBD as concept to JTBD as analytical discipline.

bland-2019.title: Testing Business Ideas: A Field Guide for Rapid Experimentation
bland-2019.note: Application. The Strategyzer-Precoil collaboration on disciplined experimentation. The methodology for testing assumptions before committing resources, drawing on lean startup and design thinking traditions but with greater rigour. This bibliography draws on training in Testing Business Ideas with David J. Bland directly.

maurya-2012.title: Running Lean: Iterate from Plan A to a Plan That Works (2nd ed)
maurya-2012.note: Application. The Lean Canvas, Maurya's adaptation of the BMC for early-stage ventures, with explicit problem-solution-fit emphasis. Useful as a complementary tool for very early-stage strategic conversations.

eyal-2014.title: Hooked: How to Build Habit-Forming Products
eyal-2014.note: Application. The Hook Model, useful as a structural framework for understanding how product-design choices affect user engagement and behaviour. Cited here with the explicit caveat that CDP's product orientation is the opposite of the addictive-engagement design Eyal documents; the framework is useful for understanding the territory CDP has chosen not to occupy.

erhard-2012.title: Creating leaders: An ontological/phenomenological model
erhard-2012.source: In S. Snook, N. Nohria & R. Khurana (Eds.), The Handbook for Teaching Leadership. SAGE Publications, Thousand Oaks CA, pp. 245-262
erhard-2012.note: Application. Erhard's academic-press chapter, co-authored with Michael Jensen of Harvard Business School. The cleanest single statement of the ontological-phenomenological framework underlying the est-Forum-Landmark lineage. The most defensible academic citation for the constructivist meaning-making position.

bartley-1978.title: Werner Erhard: The Transformation of a Man, the Founding of est
bartley-1978.note: Application. Bartley was a philosopher of science (Karl Popper's student); his biographical and intellectual treatment of Erhard remains the most rigorous historical reference for the founding of est and the philosophical lineage feeding it.

heidegger-1927.title: Being and Time (J
heidegger-1927.source: Macquarrie & E. Robinson, Trans.). Harper & Row, New York
heidegger-1927.note: Application. The philosophical source of the thrownness and authentic-resolve framework that Erhard drew on heavily in constructing the est-Forum methodology. Required for any serious account of where the Landmark constructivist position originates.

wittgenstein-1953.title: Philosophical Investigations (G.E.M
wittgenstein-1953.note: Application. The later Wittgenstein on language as constitutive of world rather than merely descriptive of it. Foundational for the constructivist position that the words we use to interpret events shape what those events become.

searle-1969.title: Speech Acts: An Essay in the Philosophy of Language
searle-1969.note: Application. Searle on the world-creating function of speech acts (declarations, commitments, promises). The Landmark methodology draws explicitly on this lineage in its account of how language creates possibility rather than merely describing it.

maturana-1980.title: Autopoiesis and Cognition: The Realization of the Living
maturana-1980.note: Application. The autopoietic theory of cognition. Establishes that perception is structure-determined: the system's organisation determines what counts as a perturbation, not the world itself. Provides biological-systems grounding for the constructivist position that Landmark articulates as the already-always listening.

mcadams-1993.title: The Stories We Live By: Personal Myths and the Making of the Self
mcadams-1993.note: Application. McAdams' founding text on narrative identity. Establishes that the self is constituted through the ongoing story we tell about who we have been, who we are, and who we are becoming. Closest empirical cousin to the Erhard meaning-making framework within mainstream personality psychology.

bruner-1991.title: The narrative construction of reality
bruner-1991.note: Application. Bruner on narrative as the fundamental mode of cognition for human meaning-making. The cognitive-science complement to McAdams' personality-psychology treatment. Background for the claim that the morning Compass entry is, among other things, a small narrative-identity act.

singer-2004.title: Narrative identity and meaning making across the adult lifespan: An introduction
singer-2004.note: Application. Singer's introduction to the special-issue treatment of narrative identity in adulthood. The contemporary research-literature grounding for the longitudinal claims about how meaning-making changes shape across a life.

pressman-1993.title: Outrageous Betrayal: The Dark Journey of Werner Erhard from est to Exile
pressman-1993.note: Application. The principal critical biography. Cited here in service of intellectual honesty: the Erhard lineage has substantial academic and journalistic critiques alongside its practitioner literature, and the bibliography that draws on the lineage owes the reader the complete picture rather than the curated one.

sceptical_balance_note: For contested claims the Science voice MUST cite both supporting AND sceptical literature where they exist. CRITICAL FRAMING: the menstrual cycle bibliography demonstrates the right discipline. Jang 2025 (cycle/cognition null) is included, BUT it is one bounded null among many positive findings on systems the cycle DOES affect: HPA reactivity (Klusmann 2023), basal cortisol (Klusmann 2022), pain perception (Riley 1999, Morales-Lalaguna 2025), sleep architecture (Baker & Lee 2023), PMDD/PMS emotion regulation (Lange 2024), PNI/immune (Bower & Kuhlman 2023, Mengelkoch 2023). The honest claim is: the cycle modulates the systems within which cognition operates - mood, sleep, autonomic state, pain perception, stress reactivity, inflammation - even when objective cognitive performance on standardised tasks holds steady. Other sceptical entries remain available where the topic warrants: carlson-1985 (astrology null), van-dam-2018 (mindfulness critique), lee-pittler-ernst-2008 (Reiki null), burkert-1972 (Pythagorean origins).
